theory.intro: To multiply a decimal by a single-digit number, multiply as if the decimal were a whole number, then put the decimal point back so the answer has the same number of decimal places.
facts: Estimate first: $3.5 \times 2 \approx 4 \times 2 = 8$, so the answer is near $7$ — sensible.
steps: Ignore the point and multiply the digits as whole numbers.
Count the decimal places in the original decimal.
Place the point in the answer so it has the same number of decimal places.
